message.content: Adversaries may gather information about a victim's hosts to aid in targeting and further attacks. This information can include administrative data (e.g., name, IP address, functionality) and configuration specifics (e.g., operating system, language). Common methods for collecting this data include Active Scanning, Phishing for Information, and compromising websites to embed malicious content. Adversaries may also leverage publicly accessible data sets, such as social media or victim-owned websites. The gathered information can facilitate additional reconnaissance, resource development, and initial access tactics, such as Supply Chain Compromise or exploiting External Remote Services. Mitigation strategies include network segmentation, regular patching, monitoring for unusual scanning activity, and educating users on phishing risks.
